mission.title: kpt: Epic: WYSIWYG Kubernetes Application Configuration
mission.description: Epic: WYSIWYG Kubernetes Application Configuration. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check kpt installation
steps.description:
Verify your kpt installation:
```bash
kpt version
```
Install if needed: see https://kpt.dev/installation/. kpt is a Kubernetes package management CLI.

steps.title: Review kpt configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kpt configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kpt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kpt
kubectl get configmap -n kpt -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kpt
```
Most Kubernetes users are interested in configuring applications. That's the primary original purpose of Kubernetes, running containerized workloads. Even cluster services / add-ons are applications.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Epic: WYSIWYG Kubernetes Application Configuration
steps.description:
Though we are using off-the-shelf apps as example app configurations, an argument in favor of starting with bespoke apps for the UI-based demo is that we wouldn't need to build up a huge catalog of apps, helm is less dominant, the solution space is most fragmented, and UIs are at least sometimes used to author resources, which maybe has some benefit of familiarity. Obviously that's also where some users want higher-level abstractions, PaaSes, and so on. And CI/CD.

It also helps me to think

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kpt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kpt
kubectl get events -n kpt --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Epic: WYSIWYG Kubernetes Application Configuration" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Though we are using off-the-shelf apps as example app configurations, an argument in favor of starting with bespoke apps for the UI-based demo is that we wouldn't need to build up a huge catalog of apps, helm is less dominant, the solution space is most fragmented, and UIs are at least sometimes used to author resources, which maybe has some benefit of familiarity.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kpt installed or the issue environment reproducible.
